schema.description: OAuth2 JWT Bearer Grant Type Issuer Trust Relationship

allow_any_subject.description: The "allow_any_subject" indicates that the issuer is allowed to have any principal as the subject of the JWT.

created_at.description: The "created_at" indicates, when grant was created.

expires_at.description: The "expires_at" indicates, when grant will expire, so we will reject assertion from "issuer" targeting "subject".

issuer.description: The "issuer" identifies the principal that issued the JWT assertion (same as "iss" claim in JWT).

public_key.description: OAuth2 JWT Bearer Grant Type Issuer Trusted JSON Web Key

kid.description: The "key_id" is key unique identifier (same as kid header in jws/jwt).

set.description: The "set" is basically a name for a group(set) of keys. Will be the same as "issuer" in grant.

public_key.title: trustedOAuth2JwtGrantJsonWebKey

scope.description: The "scope" contains list of scope values (as described in Section 3.3 of OAuth 2.0 [RFC6749])

subject.description: The "subject" identifies the principal that is the subject of the JWT.

schema.title: trustedOAuth2JwtGrantIssuer

schema.description: Error responses are sent when an error (e.g. unauthorized, bad request, ...) occurred.

debug.description:
Debug information

This field is often not exposed to protect against leaking
sensitive information.
debug.example: SQL field "foo" is not a bool.

debug.description: Debug contains debug information. This is usually not available and has to be enabled.
debug.example: The database adapter was unable to find the element

error.example: The requested resource could not be found

error_description.description: Description contains further information on the nature of the error.
error_description.example: Object with ID 12345 does not exist

id.description:
ID is a unique error ID.
feature_not_available ErrFeatureNotAvailable
quota_exceeded ErrQuotaExceeded

status_code.description: Code represents the error status code (404, 403, 401, ...).

id.description:
The error ID

Useful when trying to identify various errors in application logic.

message.example: The resource could not be found

reason.description: A human-readable reason for the error
reason.example: User with ID 1234 does not exist.

request.description:
The request ID

The request ID is often exposed internally in order to trace
errors across service architectures. This is often a UUID.

schema.title: Error response
